title: skopeo copy doesn't copy image signature into local directory
body: I'm trying to use skopeo to sign the container image. JFrog Artifactoy is the container registry. below steps are we have done **Sign Container Image from within a RHEL8 container** `skopeo copy docker://https://artifactory-registry/image:unsiged docker://https://artifactory-registry/image:signed --sign-by ${gpgfingerprint} ` ## Sign a Image `skopeo copy docker://https://artifactory-registry/image:signed dir:/imagesigned` ## Pull Signed Image to local directory I can find signature in the local directory via `ls :/imagesigned ` **Copy Signed Image into Local Directory on my MacOS, But signature doesn't appear** skopeo copy docker://https://artifactory-registry/image:signed dir:/imagesigned` I can't find the signature in the local directory of my MacOS I didn't find skopeo sign docs. Need someone's help to find what's the problem. many thanks!

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

> `docker://https:`

(That is, or should be, invalid syntax that is rejected. Assuming a copy-paste error while writing the report.)

---

Is the macOS system configured to access the same sigstore? See `registries.d/*.yaml`. `skopeo --debug` shows where it reads these config files, and what “signature storage configuration” is used.

@mtrmac, thanks for your reply. I try to do the copy in a RHEL8 VM (not from within a container), still can not copy the signature into the local directory. below is the --debug log. seems it is reading signature from the local directory which I think it should be downloaded from remote registry.

>```
>No signature storage configuration found for tools.boomerangplatform.net:8500/boomerangcicdteam/springboot-checkout:master-4-1, using built-in default file: ///var/lib/containers/sigstore
>```

Signatures are not stored in registries (unless the registry specifically supports X-Registry-Supports-Signatures, like the OpenShift integrated registry; I don’t know if any other registry supports that).

You need to set up a process to publish the signatures created locally when signing, and to configure clients to access those published signatures. Compare maybe https://gist.github.com/saschagrunert/1d0b3cc40ce0418558d7c2a5a8fe8a1c .

(The description now says something about copying files; was that edited later?) For local files, note that the debug log shows the exact location where it is looking for signatures , see the line ending with `/signature-1`.

The “Reading …-1” line means that file does not exist.
Thanks for all your reply, now I understand it, close this issue.
